name: Xylitol
notes: Causes rapid insulin release and can lead to liver failure. Even small amounts are life-threatening.

name: Grapes & Raisins
notes: Can cause acute kidney failure. The toxic compound is unknown, so all amounts should be treated as dangerous.

name: Chocolate / Theobromine
notes: Theobromine causes vomiting, seizures, and heart arrhythmia. Dark chocolate and baking chocolate are most dangerous.

name: Onion & Alliums
notes: Damage red blood cells, causing hemolytic anemia. Powdered forms are more concentrated and more dangerous.

name: Macadamia Nuts
notes: Causes weakness, vomiting, tremors, and hyperthermia within 12 hours of ingestion.

name: Caffeine
notes: Stimulates the nervous system. Causes rapid heart rate, tremors, and seizures.

name: Alcohol
notes: Even small amounts can cause vomiting, disorientation, breathing difficulties, and coma.

name: Avocado
notes: Contains persin, which can cause vomiting and diarrhea. The pit is also a choking hazard.

name: Nutmeg
notes: Contains myristicin, which causes hallucinations, disorientation, increased heart rate, and seizures in large amounts.

name: Hops
notes: Causes malignant hyperthermia (dangerously high body temperature) in dogs, which can be fatal.

name: Artificial Sweeteners (non-xylitol)
notes: Most sugar alcohols cause gastrointestinal upset in dogs. Erythritol may have similar effects to xylitol — use caution.

name: Excess Salt / Sodium
notes: Large amounts cause sodium ion poisoning — vomiting, diarrhea, tremors, and seizures. A concern in very salty foods.

name: Raw Yeast Dough
notes: Raw yeast dough can expand in the stomach and produce alcohol as it ferments. Baked products with yeast are generally safe.

name: Black Walnuts
notes: Black walnuts (not English walnuts) are toxic to dogs and cause neurological symptoms. Moldy walnuts are also dangerous.

name: Star Anise
notes: Can cause gastrointestinal distress and neurological effects in dogs in large amounts.
